subagents.title: W2-AG Scoring Engineer
subagents.system_instruction: You are a scoring/evaluation systems engineer. Design per-agent scoring schemas and statistically sound reporting bundles. Prioritize deterministic outputs, payload backward compatibility, and explicit edge-case tests. You must specify exact response fields for server payloads and SSE emission.

parsed_output.summary: Extend evaluation system with per-agent scoring, reliability adjustments, and statistical reporting bundles

code_changes.purpose: Add new message schemas for agent scoring and reporting bundle
operations: Add class AgentScore with fields: agent_id, score, criteria_breakdown, hard_gate_failures
Add class ReportingBundle with fields: mean, median, std_dev, p25, p75, n_samples
Extend EvaluationResultPayload with optional fields: agent_scores, reporting_bundle, reliability_adjustment_factor
code_changes.compatibility_notes: Existing EvaluationResultPayload fields unchanged; new fields are optional with None defaults

code_changes.purpose: Implement scoring calculations, reliability adjustments, and reporting bundle generation
operations: Add function _calculate_agent_scores(evaluations) → List[AgentScore]
Add function _apply_reliability_adjustment(scores, error_rate) → adjusted_scores
Add function _generate_reporting_bundle(scores) → ReportingBundle
Modify run_evaluation() to call new functions and populate extended payload
code_changes.compatibility_notes: Existing evaluation flow unchanged; new logic only activates when new fields requested

code_changes.purpose: Add SSE emission for real-time scoring updates
operations: Add _emit_agent_score_sse(agent_id, score_breakdown) to EvaluationOrchestrator
Modify evaluate_workflow_iteration() to emit SSE after each agent evaluation
code_changes.compatibility_notes: SSE events use new event type 'agent_score'; existing SSE channels unchanged

tests.name: test_agent_scores_payload_backward_compatible
tests.asserts: Existing EvaluationResultPayload instantiation without new fields succeeds and serializes

tests.name: test_reliability_adjustment_edge_cases
tests.asserts: _apply_reliability_adjustment returns original scores when error_rate=0, handles error_rate>=1.0

tests.name: test_reporting_bundle_statistical_correctness
tests.asserts: _generate_reporting_bundle produces correct mean, std_dev, percentiles for varied score lists

tests.name: test_sse_emission_agent_scores
tests.asserts: SSE stream contains 'agent_score' events with proper schema during evaluation run

risk_register.risk: Payload size increase causing performance degradation
risk_register.mitigation: Make agent_scores optional; add pagination for large score lists in future
risk_register.validation: Load test with 1000 agent scores; verify response time <2s p95

risk_register.risk: Statistical calculations producing incorrect values due to floating-point errors
risk_register.mitigation: Use decimal.Decimal for percentile calculations; add rounding to 4 decimal places
risk_register.validation: Compare reporting bundle outputs against numpy reference implementation

acceptance_checks: GET /api/evaluation/results/{run_id} returns extended payload with agent_scores field
SSE stream includes event: agent_score with valid JSON payload
All existing evaluation tests pass without modification
Reporting bundle stats match manual calculation for test dataset

subagents.system_instruction: You are an integration adapter engineer. Design framework adapters with strict contract conformance and graceful optional dependencies. Explicitly define fallback behavior when the Microsoft SDK is unavailable.

parsed_output.summary: Design optional Microsoft Agent Framework adapter with strict contract conformance and explicit fallback behavior

code_changes.purpose: Create adapter with optional SDK dependency, canonical event mapping, and explicit fallback
operations: Create class MicrosoftAgentFrameworkAdapter with __init__ checking SDK availability via try/except import
Add property _sdk_available returning bool, property _fallback_mode returning bool
Implement send_event() with guard clause: if not _sdk_available, log warning and return None
Add _map_to_canonical_event() converting internal event dict to Microsoft SDK event object format
Add class method is_available() returning bool for runtime dependency checks
code_changes.compatibility_notes: Adapter fails gracefully when SDK missing (returns None, logs warning). No breaking changes to existing callers.

code_changes.purpose: Export adapter with availability check for optional integration
operations: Add conditional export: if MicrosoftAgentFrameworkAdapter.is_available(): register adapter in INTEGRATION_REGISTRY
Add fallback placeholder entry in registry when unavailable with metadata {'available': False}
code_changes.compatibility_notes: Registry maintains backward compatibility, existing integrations unchanged.

code_changes.purpose: Extend EvaluationResult payload schema for agent_scores with optional microsoft_agent_framework field
operations: Add optional field 'microsoft_agent_framework' to AgentScores dataclass with default None
Add comment documenting field requires optional SDK dependency
code_changes.compatibility_notes: Additive schema evolution, existing serialization unchanged.

risk_register.validation: Test with mocked broken import and verify fallback mode activates

risk_register.validation: Test with multiple mocked SDK versions and verify mapping compatibility

risk_register.risk: Silent failure in fallback mode may hide integration issues
risk_register.mitigation: Explicit warning log at WARNING level when fallback activates, include in health checks
risk_register.validation: Verify logs contain specific warning when SDK unavailable

acceptance_checks: Adapter instantiation succeeds with and without Microsoft SDK installed
send_event() returns None and logs warning when SDK unavailable
INTEGRATION_REGISTRY shows available=False when dependency missing
